heading: Definitions
label: 951 Definitions
notes: Amendments2015— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section defined terms for this chapter.Pub. L. 114–811962—Subsec. (e). substituted definition of “United States” for definition of “enforcement agency”.Pub. L. 87–814

Effective Date, , provided: The Costa Rican convention was ratified on , and the Mexican convention on . Therefore, the act took effect upon its approval on .Act Sept. 7, 1950, ch. 907, § 1464 Stat. 780“This Act [this chapter] shall take effect with respect to each of the conventions upon the entry into force of that convention, unless such entry into force shall be prior to the date of approval of this Act [] in which case this Act [this chapter] shall take effect immediately.”Sept. 7, 1950March 3, 1950July 11, 1950Sept. 7, 1950

Short Title of 2015 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–81, title II, § 201129 Stat. 660Nov. 5, 2015“This title [amending sections 951 to 953, 955, 957, 959, and 962 of this title and repealing chapter 16B of this title and provisions set out as a note under ] may be cited as the ‘Antigua Convention Implementing Act of 2015’.”section 972 of this title
Short Title, , provided:Act Sept. 7, 1950, ch. 907, § 164 Stat. 777“That this Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Tuna Conventions Act of 1950’.”

Separability, , provided:Act Sept. 7, 1950, ch. 907, § 1364 Stat. 780“If any provision of this Act [this chapter] or the application of such provision to any circumstances or persons shall be held invalid, the validity of the remainder of the Act and the applicability of such provision to other circumstances or persons shall not be affected thereby.”

children.heading: Antigua Convention
children.label: 1 Antigua Convention
children.text: The term “Antigua Convention” means the Convention for the Strengthening of the Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission Established by the 1949 Convention Between the United States of America and the Republic of Costa Rica, signed at Washington, .November 14, 2003

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission provided for by the Convention.

children.label: A
children.text: the Convention for the Establishment of an Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission, signed at Washington, , by the United States of America and the Republic of Costa Rica;May 31, 1949

children.label: B
children.text: the Antigua Convention, upon its entry into force for the United States, and any amendments thereto that are in force for the United States; or

children.label: C
children.text: both such Conventions, as the context requires.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 4 Person
children.text: The term “person” means an individual, partnership, corporation, or association subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 5 United States
children.text: The term “United States” includes all areas under the sovereignty of the United States.

children.heading: United States Commissioners
children.label: 6 United States Commissioners
children.text: The term “United States commissioners” means the individuals appointed in accordance with .1section 952(a) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be capitalized.1
